summary: Step 17 of the welder setup instructions describes DCEP (Direct Current Electrode Positive) wire setup for Solid Core (gas shielded) welding. It covers plugging cable polarity, choosing shielding gas, securing the gas cylinder, purging the valve, attaching the regulator, and connecting the gas hose, with an accompanying diagram of cable socket polarity and a sequence of cylinder/regulator setup illustrations.
text:
17. DCEP Direct Current Electrode Positive Wire Setup for Solid Core (gas shielded) welding:

a. Plug Ground Clamp Cable into Negative (–) Socket. Plug Wire Feed Power Cable into Positive (+) Socket. Twist cables clockwise all the way to lock in place.

b. Determine which type of shielding gas would be appropriate for the welding you will do. Refer to the Settings Chart on the inside of the Welder door.

c. With assistance, set the cylinder (not included) onto a cabinet or cart near the Welder and secure the cylinder in place with two straps (not included) to prevent tipping.

d. Remove the cylinder's cap. Stand to the side of the valve opening, then open the valve briefly to blow dust and dirt from the valve opening. Close the cylinder valve.

e. Locate the Regulator (included) and close its valve until it is loose, then thread Regulator onto cylinder and wrench-tighten connection.

Note: When using C100 shielding gas, connect the enclosed CGA 580/320 adapter to the inlet connection of the Regulator and wrench-tighten. Thread the adapter onto the gas cylinder and wrench-tighten.

f. Attach the Gas Hose (included) to the Regulator's outlet and the Welder's gas inlet. Wrench-tighten both connections.

[Diagram labels: DCEP Solid Core (Gas Shielded) Polarity Setup; Ground Clamp Cable in Negative Socket; Wire Feed Power Cable in Positive Socket]

[Rating plate visible on welder: Vulcan OMNIPRO 220, Item 57812, IP21S, Conforms to ANSI/IEC Std. 60974-1, UPC 193175422590, with duty cycle tables for U1=240V and U1=120V input, showing X (duty cycle %), I2 (output current), U2 (output voltage), U0=86V, I1max and I1eff values for various processes.]

tables.title: Rating Plate - 240V Input, Table 1 (e.g., MIG/Flux-Core range 30A/15.5V to 220A/25V)

figures.caption: DCEP Solid Core (Gas Shielded) Polarity Setup
figures.description: Illustration of the welder's front panel showing the ground clamp cable plugged into the Negative socket and the wire feed power cable plugged into the Positive socket, demonstrating correct DCEP polarity setup for solid core (gas shielded) welding.

figures.caption: Gas cylinder and regulator setup steps c-f
figures.description: Sequence of illustrations showing: (c) a gas cylinder strapped to a cart/cabinet with two straps; (d) briefly opening the cylinder valve to clean dust/dirt then closing it; (e) threading the regulator onto the cylinder and wrench-tightening; (f) attaching the gas hose from the regulator outlet to the welder's gas inlet and wrench-tightening both connections.

key_facts: Step 17 covers DCEP (Direct Current Electrode Positive) wire setup for Solid Core (gas shielded) welding.
Ground Clamp Cable plugs into the Negative (–) Socket for DCEP setup.
Wire Feed Power Cable plugs into the Positive (+) Socket for DCEP setup.
Cables should be twisted clockwise all the way to lock in place.
Gas cylinder (not included) should be secured with two straps (not included) to prevent tipping.
Cylinder valve should be briefly opened to blow dust/dirt from the valve opening before attaching the regulator, then closed.
When using C100 shielding gas, an enclosed CGA 580/320 adapter must be connected to the regulator inlet and threaded onto the gas cylinder, both wrench-tightened.
Gas Hose (included) connects the Regulator's outlet to the Welder's gas inlet; both connections must be wrench-tightened.
Welder model is Vulcan OMNIPRO 220, Item 57812.
Welder has IP21S ingress protection rating and conforms to ANSI/IEC Std. 60974-1.
At 240V input, output current range is 30A/15.5V to 220A/25V with duty cycles of 25% (200A/24V), 60% (130A/20.5V), and 100% (115A/19.75V).
At 240V input, I1max values are 25.5A, 23.7A, 15.6A and I1eff values are 12.8A, 11.9A, 8.5A depending on process.
At 120V input, output current range is 30A/15.5V to 140A/21V with duty cycles of 40% (100A/19V), 60% (85A/18.25V), and 100% (75A/17.75V).
At 120V input, I1max values are 20.8A, 19.5A, 20.6A and I1eff values are 13.1A, 12.3A, 13A depending on process.
No-load voltage U0 = 86V across all rated duty cycle tables on the rating plate.
